notes: No information found on a cricketer named 'Ravish Sharma' playing for India.

Virat Kohli retired from T20Is in June 2024 and from Test cricket in May 2025 but continues to play ODIs and in the IPL. ([skysports.com](https://www.skysports.com/cricket/news/12344/13161057/indias-virat-kohli-and-rohit-sharma-retire-from-t20-international-cricket-after-world-cup-win-over-south-africa?utm_source=openai))

Rohit Sharma retired from T20Is in 2024 and Test cricket in 2025 but continues to play ODIs and domestic leagues. [Source: Sky Sports](https://www.skysports.com/cricket/news/12123/13363381/rohit-sharma-india-captain-announces-retirement-from-test-cricket)

MS Dhoni retired from international cricket on August 15, 2020, but continues to play in the IPL for Chennai Super Kings. ([timesofindia.indiatimes.com](https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/sports/cricket/news/watch-when-ms-dhoni-said-goodbye-to-international-cricket-on-independence-day/articleshow/102740996.cms?utm_source=openai))

David Warner retired from all international cricket formats in 2024 but continues to participate in domestic and franchise leagues. [Source: Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/David_Warner_%28cricketer%29)

Steve Smith retired from ODI cricket in March 2025 but continues to play Test and T20I cricket for Australia. Source: https://www.espn.com/cricket/story/_/id/44116384/australia-news-steven-smith-retires-odi-cricket

AB de Villiers retired from all forms of international cricket in 2018 and from all forms of cricket in 2021. ([icc-cricket.com](https://www.icc-cricket.com/news/ab-de-villiers-announces-retirement-from-all-forms?utm_source=openai))

As of April 2026, Babar Azam continues to play international cricket across all formats and participates in domestic leagues. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Babar_Azam?utm_source=openai))

Kane Williamson retired from T20 internationals in November 2025 to focus on Test and ODI cricket. ([skysports.com](https://www.skysports.com/cricket/news/12040/13462468/kane-williamson-former-new-zealand-captain-retires-from-t20-internationals-to-focus-on-test-cricket?utm_source=openai))

As of June 2023, Chris Gayle has not officially announced his retirement from international cricket, expressing concerns about the future of the game. [Source](https://crickettimes.com/2023/06/chris-gayle-voiced-his-concerns-about-the-current-state-and-future-of-international-cricket/)
